title: Find Elements by Role
description: Master Playwright's role-based locators for accessible and reliable element selection
objectives: Use getByRole for different element types
Find buttons, links, and headings by role
instructions:
Write a Playwright test that:
1. Navigates to a webpage
2. Finds and clicks a button using getByRole
3. Finds a link using getByRole
4. Finds a heading using getByRole
5. Verifies each element exists

starterCode.python:
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect

def test_find_by_role(page: Page):
    page.goto('https://playwright.dev')
    
    # TODO: Find and click 'Get started' link by role
    
    # TODO: Find the main heading by role
    
    # TODO: Assert heading contains 'Playwright'
    
    # TODO: Find 'Node.js' button by role
    pass

solution.python:
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect
import re

def test_find_by_role(page: Page):
    page.goto('https://playwright.dev')
    
    get_started = page.get_by_role('link', name='Get started')
    expect(get_started).to_be_visible()
    
    heading = page.get_by_role('heading', level=1)
    expect(heading).to_contain_text('Playwright')
    
    node_button = page.get_by_role('button', name=re.compile('node', re.I))
    expect(node_button).to_be_visible()

hints: Use { name: 'text' } to filter by accessible name
Role-based locators are more resilient to DOM changes
